message:
test(scheduler): Add pop_with_lane and steal_ready_batch_into tests (bd-2tlx)

555 lines of new priority scheduler tests:

pop_with_lane tests:
- pop_with_lane_returns_cancel_lane: cancel tasks dispatch from Cancel lane
- pop_with_lane_returns_timed_lane: timed tasks dispatch from Timed lane
- pop_with_lane_returns_ready_lane: ready tasks dispatch from Ready lane
- pop_with_lane_respects_lane_ordering: Cancel > Timed > Ready priority,
  then None when empty
- pop_with_lane_rng_tiebreak_among_equal_priority: different rng_hint
  values produce different orderings among equal-priority tasks

steal_ready_batch_into tests:
- steal_ready_batch_into_fills_buffer: steals up to half of ready tasks
- Additional work-stealing edge case coverage

These tests verify the three-lane priority invariant (Cancel > Timed >
Ready) that is foundational to cancellation correctness — cancel work
must always dispatch before new ready work to ensure drain progress.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
